notes: These evals test the TICK DECISION behavior. A real tick needs live GitHub state and the Cron tools, so each prompt hands the model a concrete slot + provider state and asks for the step-by-step plan it would execute; assertions check the plan takes the branch the contract mandates. Run via skill-creator's eval runner — CI does not execute these (ci.yml gates on vitest/check-skill-deps; skill-eval.yml runs gate scenarios sourced from muggle-ai-brain and the routing eval).

evals.prompt: Tick muggle-ai-works-pr500 500. The slot is open. The PR has two unresolved, not-outdated review threads whose newest comments are from a human (no muggle-do:bot marker), CI is fully green, and the branch is level with master. Walk me through the plan for this tick.

assertions.name: computes_actionable_from_live_thread_state
assertions.text: Plan derives the actionable set from current unresolved-thread state, not from a stored review-id cursor.

assertions.name: classifies_by_loop_marker
assertions.text: Plan decides a thread is actionable by the absence of the muggle-do:bot marker on the newest comment, never by author login.

assertions.name: cancels_own_cron_before_dispatch
assertions.text: Plan cancels this watcher's own cron before dispatching, so no tick overlaps the running cycle.

assertions.name: dispatches_address_reviews
assertions.text: Plan dispatches /muggle-do with an address-reviews directive carrying the PR URL, slug, and owning review ids, then exits.

assertions.name: stays_a_dumb_pipe
assertions.text: Plan does NOT classify reviews, post replies, resolve threads, or escalate — those belong to /muggle-do.

evals.prompt: Tick muggle-ai-works-pr501 501. The slot is open. There is one unresolved human review thread AND two red required checks on the head SHA. Walk me through the plan for this tick.

assertions.name: dispatches_address_reviews_only
assertions.text: Plan dispatches address-reviews for the review thread.

assertions.name: does_not_poll_or_dispatch_ci
assertions.text: Plan does NOT dispatch fix-ci on this tick — actionable feedback preempts the CI branch entirely.

assertions.name: single_dispatch_then_exit
assertions.text: Plan performs exactly one dispatch and exits rather than handling both concerns in one tick.

evals.prompt: Tick muggle-ai-works-pr502 502. The slot is open, there are no unresolved review threads, CI is green. GitHub reports mergeStateStatus BLOCKED and mergeable MERGEABLE, and the compare call shows behind_by 4. Walk me through the plan for this tick.

assertions.name: reads_staleness_from_compare
assertions.text: Plan determines the branch is out of date from behind_by on the compare call (commit ancestry).

assertions.name: does_not_trust_mergeStateStatus
assertions.text: Plan does NOT conclude the branch is current just because mergeStateStatus reads BLOCKED rather than BEHIND.

assertions.name: dispatches_rebase
assertions.text: Plan dispatches /muggle-do with a rebase directive (PR URL + slug, no review ids, no check names).

assertions.name: keys_dedup_on_head_and_base_pair
assertions.text: Plan keys the rebase attempt/escalation lookup on the head..base_tip pair, not the head SHA alone.

evals.prompt: Tick muggle-ai-works-pr503 503. The slot is open, no unresolved review threads, branch is level with its base. Two checks are red on the head SHA. ci_fix_attempts for that SHA is 1 and the SHA is not in ci_escalated_shas. Walk me through the plan for this tick.

assertions.name: checks_attempt_budget
assertions.text: Plan verifies ci_fix_attempts for the head SHA is under the cap of 3 before dispatching.

assertions.name: checks_escalated_set
assertions.text: Plan verifies the head SHA is not in ci_escalated_shas.

assertions.name: dispatches_fix_ci_with_check_names
assertions.text: Plan dispatches /muggle-do with a fix-ci directive carrying the red check names.

assertions.name: cancels_own_cron_before_dispatch
assertions.text: Plan cancels this watcher's cron before dispatching.

evals.prompt: Tick muggle-ai-works-pr504 504. The slot is open, no unresolved review threads, branch is level with its base. Three checks are red on the head SHA, ci_fix_attempts for that SHA is 3, and the SHA is in ci_escalated_shas. Walk me through the plan for this tick.

assertions.name: does_not_dispatch
assertions.text: Plan does NOT dispatch fix-ci — the budget is spent and the SHA is already escalated.

assertions.name: treats_as_durable_block
assertions.text: Plan classifies this idle as blocked pending a human (reason ci_escalated), not as a transient idle.

assertions.name: reminds_owner_at_1m
assertions.text: Plan emits a one-line owner reminder and keeps the normal 1m cadence — it does not back off, slow down, or stop polling.

assertions.name: no_pr_side_post
assertions.text: Plan does NOT post anything to the PR for a blocked tick; the reminder goes to the loop owner in-session.

evals.prompt: Tick muggle-ai-works-pr505 505. Refreshing the PR shows state MERGED. The slot on disk still says open. Walk me through the plan for this tick.

assertions.name: finalizes_the_slot
assertions.text: Plan finalizes the slot: marks it terminal, writes result.md, logs, and unschedules this watcher's cron.

assertions.name: does_not_respawn
assertions.text: Plan does NOT respawn or re-arm a watcher — a terminal PR needs none.

assertions.name: hands_off_terminal_wrapup
assertions.text: Plan hands the terminal wrap-up to /muggle-do post-merge cleanup as the turn's last action.

assertions.name: unschedules_recorded_id_first
assertions.text: Plan cancels the cron by the id recorded in cron.json first, falling back to a CronList match — so teardown works even after CronList has gone blind.

evals.prompt: Tick muggle-ai-works-pr506 506. Reading the slot, prs.json already records state merged and result.md exists — this fire was queued before the cron was cancelled. Walk me through the plan for this tick.

assertions.name: detects_stale_fire
assertions.text: Plan recognises this as a stale queued fire from the on-disk terminal state before fetching anything.

assertions.name: does_not_refetch_or_refinalize
assertions.text: Plan does NOT re-fetch the PR, re-write result.md, or re-run the terminal handoff.

assertions.name: defensively_cancels_and_logs
assertions.text: Plan defensively cancels any lingering cron for the slug and appends a stale-tick line to followup.log, then exits.

evals.prompt: Tick muggle-ai-works-pr507 507. prs.json records state merged and result.md exists. followup.log already holds four stale-tick lines for this slot and no stale-orphan-escalated line. Walk me through the plan for this tick.

assertions.name: detects_orphaned_cron
assertions.text: Plan concludes the cron is orphaned — repeated stale fires after finalize mean both cancel-cron lookups (recorded id, CronList match) keep missing it — rather than treating this as a normal queued-fire drain.

assertions.name: escalates_to_owner_once
assertions.text: Plan surfaces a one-line owner notice that only a session restart clears the orphan, and appends a stale-orphan-escalated marker line so the notice never repeats.

assertions.name: later_fires_absorb_silently
assertions.text: Plan treats any stale fire after the marker exists as a silent absorb — log stale-tick and exit, no repeated notice.

assertions.name: never_guess_deletes_other_ids
assertions.text: Plan does NOT delete cron ids that CronList surfaces for other slugs — those belong to other live watchers.

evals.prompt: Arm the watch for muggle-ai-works-pr508 508 after a clean drain (no reviews, no unresolved threads, checks green at seed time). Describe the watch.sh monitor loop you would write and what makes it wake the session.

assertions.name: polls_check_run_rollup
assertions.text: The loop polls the head SHA's check-run rollup each iteration, not just reviews and threads.

assertions.name: wakes_only_on_settled_red
assertions.text: The loop wakes the session when the checks have settled with a failure (no check pending and one or more in the fail bucket) — it does NOT wake while any check is still pending.

assertions.name: dedups_ci_on_head_sha
assertions.text: The loop dedups the CI wake on the head SHA (fires once per red head) rather than on a monotonic id, because the check rollup is non-monotonic and resets on each push.

assertions.name: stays_a_dumb_pipe
assertions.text: The loop only signals that CI went red and hands off to a tick; it does NOT apply the ci_fix_attempts budget, classify the failure, or dispatch fix-ci itself — those belong to the tick and /muggle-do.
